meta.purpose: Fitness-function corpus for an OpenRewrite recipe migrating Java 8/11/17 codebases (and their popular dependencies) to Java 21.
meta.verification_method: For each repo we fetched the actual pom.xml or build.gradle (and where relevant build.gradle.kts / libs.versions.toml / versions.props) from raw.githubusercontent.com on the listed branch_or_tag, confirmed the declared Java target version (java.version / maven.compiler.source / sourceCompatibility / minJavaVersion), and confirmed the presence and version of the target dependency family. Commit SHA captured at verification time.
known_gaps: spring-boot-2 x java17: only 1 verified repo (jeecgboot). The 2.7-on-17 combination is empirically rare in public OSS because most teams jumped from Boot 2.x/Java 11 straight to Boot 3.x/Java 17.

junit4-mockito x java17: the only widely-found family is the Jenkins core + plugin ecosystem (junit-vintage-engine keeps junit:junit:4.13.2 alive). The dataset uses two Jenkins LTS tags as distinct rows but they share an owner.
Several Java 17 + javax repos have a declared compiler release of 8 or 11 but RUN on Java 17. They are listed with caveats; the recipe should override compiler.release=17 when exercising those rows.

fitness_function_guidance.weighting: Weight cells by sample count and breadth-of-domain. Java 8 / Java 11 cells are well-covered and should dominate scoring. Java 17 cells are intentionally light — that pair is rare in the wild and recipe quality there can be measured against fewer but representative samples.

repos.notes: JPA-based admin RBAC backend; multi-module (system, common, logging, tools, generator). Strong javax.* migration surface, also has Lombok + Hibernate via Boot 2.7.
Distributed job scheduling middleware. powerjob-server pins springboot.version=2.7.18 + Java 1.8; uses spring-boot-starter-web/data-jpa/actuator/websocket. Real production system.
Ctrip's distributed configuration center. Tag v2.2.0 pinned because master has since moved to Boot 4. Multi-module (configservice/adminservice/portal/biz/common). Eureka + Spring Security + JPA.
Canonical Spring Petclinic reactive variant. WebFlux + Cassandra + Spring Cloud Sleuth/Zipkin. The only widely-known Java 11 + Boot 2 reference app from the Spring org.
JHipster React sample app at the last 2.7.x-line tag. No explicit spring-boot-starter-parent — Boot version comes from BOM. Java 11.
Java e-commerce platform. Multi-module Maven, non-trivial size — useful real-world Java 11 + Boot 2.5 test fixture.
Large low-code platform. java.version=17 with Boot 2.7.18. MyBatis-Plus, Shiro, Spring Cloud Alibaba. Big build — pin a sub-module for cheap recipe runs.

CAVEAT: pom declares compiler.release=11 but CI matrix and enforcer rule explicitly accept JDK 17 (and 21). Override compiler.release=17 to exercise this cell. Treat as Java 17 + Boot 2.7 test fixture with the override applied. Also valid as Java 11 row.

GAP: third sample not found. The Java 17 + Spring Boot 2.x cell is empirically thin in public OSS — most teams that adopted Java 17 jumped to Boot 3.x at the same time. Investigated and rejected: codecentric/spring-boot-admin 2.7.x (Java 8), apache/shenyu (Java 8), dromara/RuoYi-Vue-Plus v4.7.0 (Java 8), macrozheng/mall v1.0.3 (Java 8), yangzongzhuan/RuoYi-Cloud v3.6.4 (Java 8), pig-mesh/pig (Java 8), halo-dev/halo v2 (GPL). Recommend relaxing to Boot 2.6 or using forks; otherwise weight this cell lower in the fitness function.
Large reactive library. sourceCompatibility/targetCompatibility = VERSION_1_8; toolchain JavaLanguageVersion.of(8). JUnit 4 + Mockito both present and used heavily.

guava-tests/pom.xml depends on junit:junit and org.mockito:mockito-core; parent pom sets source/target 1.8. Flag: HEAD has in-progress Mockito removal in some test files, but bulk still uses it. Pin to a stable tag if you want a frozen corpus.
libs.versions.toml: junit = junit:junit:4.13.2; toolchain Java 8. Partial fit: JUnit 4 only, no Mockito dep. Strong test of JUnit4to5 recipe without confounding Mockito changes.
minJavaVersion = JavaVersion.VERSION_11. versions.props pins junit:junit=4.13.2 and org.mockito:mockito*=5.23.0. Carrotsearch randomizedtesting + JUnit 4 as primary; vintage engine wired.

Parent pom org.jenkins-ci:jenkins:1.112 sets maven.compiler.release=11. core/pom.xml declares junit-vintage-engine + mockito-core, pulling JUnit 4 transitively. Classic large-scale Java 11 + JUnit 4 + Mockito codebase.

PARTIAL FIT: JUnit 4 only, no Mockito (Lucene uses LuceneTestCase + randomizedtesting). minJavaVersion = 11. If strict junit4-mockito pairing is required, swap; otherwise an excellent JUnit-4 stress test against the migration recipe.
Parent pom jenkins-ci:jenkins:1.123 sets maven.compiler.release=17. Same JUnit-4 + Mockito-via-vintage pattern as the 2.452 row but on Java 17.

Parent pom jenkins-ci:jenkins:1.130 sets maven.compiler.release=17. Same repo as 2.479 but a later LTS tag — captures evolution. Pick one of these two for a unique-owner cell; both kept for compare-and-contrast.

GAP: third sample under a different owner not found. The Java 17 + JUnit 4 + Mockito combination is genuinely rare — by the time projects moved to Java 17 (≈2023+), they had also moved to JUnit Jupiter. Jenkins is the principal counterexample because junit-vintage-engine ships first-class. Investigated and rejected: apache/maven maven-4.0.x (Jupiter), apache/hadoop branch-3.5 (Jupiter, enforcer bans org.junit.**), apache/solr main (Java 21), apache/lucene main (Java 25). Recommend looking at the Jenkins plugin ecosystem (jenkinsci/git-plugin, jenkinsci/credentials-plugin, etc.) — many inherit the Java 17 parent and still use vintage.

repos.dep_version_observed: javax.servlet, javax.persistence, javax.ws.rs, javax.ejb, javax.inject across many modules; jakarta.xml.bind-api 2.3.2 (still javax namespace), com.sun.xml.ws:jaxws-rt 2.3.5
repos.license: NOASSERTION (CDDL/GPL per-file mix — verify before publishing derivative)
repos.notes: Canonical Java EE 7 samples covering CDI/EJB/JAX-RS/JSF/JPA/JMS/servlet. Heavy javax.* import surface across many small modules — ideal stress test for the namespace migration recipe.

repos.dep_version_observed: java.version=1.8; spring-boot 2.2.7.RELEASE, hibernate 5.4.15, javax.annotation-api, javax.cache:cache-api, javax.validation:validation-api 2.0.1.Final
repos.notes: Sample Spring Boot 2.2 + Hibernate 5 monolith. Strong real-world coverage of JPA, Bean Validation, Spring MVC servlet stack. Composite test case: also exercises hibernate-jackson-lombok recipes.

repos.dep_version_observed: org.jboss.spec:jboss-javaee-7.0 1.1.1.Final (transitively pulls javax.servlet, javax.persistence, javax.ws.rs, javax.ejb, javax.inject, javax.faces, javax.json); resteasy 3.15.3.Final
repos.notes: Antonio Goncalves' classic Java EE 7 petstore. JSF + EJB + JPA + JAX-RS + CDI all in one app. Pin a SHA before running the harness.

repos.dep_version_observed: java.version=11; spring-boot 2.7.3, hibernate 5.6.10.Final, javax.annotation-api, javax.cache:cache-api, javax.validation:validation-api 2.0.1.Final
repos.notes: Same repo as jakarta-j8-2 but the 7.9.4 tag, which is Java 11 + Spring Boot 2.7 (still javax). JPA entities verified to import javax.persistence.*.

repos.dep_version_observed: quarkus-bom 2.7.6.Final; quarkus-resteasy (javax.ws.rs); JAX-RS + CDI imports use javax.inject, javax.ws.rs.GET/Path/Produces
repos.notes: Last javax-based major version of Quarkus quickstarts (Quarkus 3 switched to jakarta). Many small JAX-RS/CDI examples — good coverage breadth.

repos.dep_version_observed: maven-compiler-plugin source/target=1.8 (compiles on 11+; Spring Boot 2.7 runtime baseline Java 11); javax.inject:javax.inject 1; jersey-* 1.19.4 (javax.ws.rs); javax.servlet via Spring Boot 2.7 transitive
repos.notes: Last release line of Spring Cloud Netflix (Eureka client/server) on javax. javax.inject explicit. Boot 2.7-based runtime.

repos.dep_version_observed: spring-boot 2.7.18, spring 5.3.31, javax-annotation-api 1.3.1; javax.servlet transitively via Boot 2.7; maven.compiler.release=11 in pom but enforcer accepts JDK 11/17/21
repos.notes: CAVEAT: pom declares compiler.release=11 but CI matrix includes Java 17. Override compiler.release=17 to exercise the cell. Also appears in sb2-j17-2 — same repo serving two cells (it is genuinely a Boot 2 + javax + Java 11/17 fixture).
CAVEAT: pom source/target=1.8 but Keycloak 19 server requires JDK 17 (WildFly distro). Override compiler.release=17 if your harness requires it. Heavy JEE deps (servlet, JAX-RS, JSON-P).

repos.branch_or_tag: pre-2023-jakarta-migration tag (verify; main is now jakarta)
repos.commit_sha: to-be-pinned (main was migrated to jakarta around May 2023 — pick the prior commit)
repos.notes: CAVEAT: requires pinning to a pre-jakarta-migration commit. The repo had a Java 17 + javax window. Treat as a known-fragile fixture — confirm the pinned SHA still compiles before using.
Self-hosted jackson sample. Jackson 2.x-line is the canonical OpenRewrite source for the jackson migration recipe. Subset: jackson family.
Heavy Jackson consumer. maven.compiler.release=8. Subset: jackson family.

repos.dep_version_observed: lombok via Boot 2.7.18 (1.18.x), hibernate 5.6.x via spring-boot-starter-data-jpa
repos.notes: Cross-listed with sb2-j8-1. Excellent multi-recipe fixture: tests Lombok + Hibernate 5→6 + Spring Boot 2→3 + javax→jakarta together. Subset: lombok + hibernate.
Parent pom: target.java.version=11. Lombok used across modules. Subset: lombok.

repos.dep_version_observed: hibernate-core, jackson-databind, jackson-core, jackson-module-kotlin (via Spring Boot 2.7.7 BOM => Hibernate 5.6.x / Jackson 2.13.x)
repos.notes: Archived repo (stable corpus). java.version=11; spring-native-configuration explicitly declares hibernate-core and jackson-databind. Subset: hibernate + jackson.

CAVEAT: pom declares maven.compiler.release=8 but project officially requires JDK 11+ runtime (WildFly-based). Hibernate 5.3.24 is a prime OpenRewrite 5→6 target — include for that signal. Subset: hibernate + jackson.

java.version=17, maven.compiler.source/target=17, Spring Boot 3.5.14, Lombok across modules. Largest e-commerce demo on GitHub. NOTE: already on Hibernate 6 — useful as 'post-migration' golden, not pre-migration input. Repurpose accordingly.

repos.dep_version_observed: jackson via spring-boot-starter-webmvc (Boot 4.0.3 => Jackson 2.18.x); Hibernate via spring-data-jpa
repos.notes: java.version=17 enforced via maven-enforcer-plugin. Canonical Spring sample. NOTE: already on the latest Boot/Hibernate — useful as 'after' state. Pin an older tag (springboot-2.7.x) if you want a pre-migration input.

repos.dep_version_observed: Jackson via Spring Boot 4.0.1 BOM; Spring Cloud 2025.1.0
repos.notes: Microservices variant: 8 modules, all on Boot 4.0 / Java 17. NOTE: post-migration state — pair with an older tag for pre/post comparison.
